title: By Thursday
caption: By the end of her first week, Chloe had settled into a rhythm she hadn't expected to find so quickly. She learned where Marco kept the croissant tongs and which display tray went where, and she arranged the morning pastries with a confidence that surprised them both. When Mr. Chen arrived for his usual order, she handed over the paper bag without fumbling, and he responded with the slow, dignified tilt of his head that she was already learning to read as high praise. Then the door burst open and Rosie arrived — brown pigtails flying, purple sneakers squeaking — and pointed straight at the glass case as if the pain au chocolat might escape. Chloe had already saved the corner table. Rosie accepted this as her due. By the time the morning rush settled and the light turned long and golden through the front window, Chloe stood behind the counter with her notebook in hand, watching Rosie eat her pastry, and felt something quiet and unexpected settle in her chest. Willowmere had not asked if she was staying. It had simply assumed she would.
story_notes: Chloe proves herself capable at the bakery; Mr. Chen approves silently; Rosie claims her corner table; Chloe feels at home.
